contextual.title: Prospective Cohort Study of the Effect of Bariatric/Metabolic Surgery on Morbid Obesity Patients With Metabolic Syndrome
contextual.text:
Summary: The purpose of this study is to determine the change in kidney function and blood pressure after gastric bypass versus conventional medical therapy in morbid obesity. The study mainly focus on glomerular filtration rate(GFR) with known relation to the renal function and 24 hours ambulatory blood pressure monitoring after intervention of gastric bypass or medical treatment.
Inclusion criteria: inclusion criteria: 

 Morbid obesity (BMI>30) patients with one of comorbidity (type 2 diabetes, dyslipidemia, or hypertension) 

 Morbid obese patients (BMI>35) 

 
Exclusion criteria: : 

 Prior bariatric surgery 

 Malignancy (any type) 

 End stage renal disease
